observation.images.image.comment: Primary slot (env agentview). float32 [0,1] CHW; the policy itself resizes to 224x224 (config resize_images_to) before the Qwen3-VL processor, so the env's native 256x256 passes through unchanged.

observation.images.image2.comment: Wrist slot (env robot0_eye_in_hand); same 'image'/'image2' naming as the pi0.5 LIBERO checkpoint.

observation.text.comment: Task instruction, formatted into the Qwen3-VL prompt template ('Your task is {instruction}. ...'); required by the VLA.

observation.state.eef_pos.comment: Absolute eef position, base frame. MEAN_STD stats from the checkpoint's normalizer safetensors (its own LIBERO finetune split; close to but not identical to the physical-intelligence/libero values).

observation.state.eef_rot.comment: Absolute eef orientation (axis-angle), base frame — same composition as the LIBERO env's state vector.

observation.state.gripper.comment: Gripper finger qpos (2 DOF). State (8-dim) feeds the DiT action head's state encoder; the full vector is MEAN_STD-normalized as one block.

action.delta_eef_pos.comment: OSC_POSE translation delta, base frame. MIN_MAX-normalized to [-1,1] during training; the postprocessor clamps the model output to [-1,1] (vla_jepa_clip_actions) before unnormalizing. min/max match the LIBERO env clip bounds.

action.delta_eef_rot.comment: OSC_POSE rotation delta (axis-angle) vs current eef. Stats verbatim from the checkpoint's normalizer safetensors; min/max match the LIBERO env clip bounds.

action.gripper.comment: Trained in {0,1} where 1=open (stats above are that space; action.mask=0 excludes it from MIN_MAX). The checkpoint's postprocessor snaps it to {0,1} (vla_jepa_pre_snap_gripper) then maps 1-2*(g>0.5) (vla_jepa_binarize_gripper), so the emitted action is exactly {-1 open, +1 close} — robosuite/LIBERO convention, no adapter work needed. ABSOLUTE (arm is delta).

comment: VLA-JEPA (Qwen3-VL-2B backbone + V-JEPA2 latent world model + DiT-B flow-matching action head; arXiv:2602.10098, LeRobot port of ginwind/VLA-JEPA) finetuned on LIBERO, one Franka Panda. Chunked inference: predict_action_chunk -> 7-step chunk, all 7 executed before re-inference (n_action_steps=7), matching the starVLA LIBERO eval client's chunk caching (no action ensembling). The world model is training-only; at inference only Qwen + action head run. State (8) is consumed by the action head's state encoder (input_features includes observation.state, unlike the SimplerEnv/Pretrain siblings). Requires a lerobot build that ships policies/vla_jepa (git main as of 2026-06; not in the 0.5.x PyPI releases).
